response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a strong web and full-stack engineer whose career has been built almost entirely on TypeScript, Node.js, DLang, and front-end technologies. His C++ exposure is limited to roughly six months at National Instruments (2014–2015) writing components for a C IDE and an academic thesis project—nowhere near the 5–10 years of hands-on C++ on Windows the role mandates. He has no demonstrable fixed-income, derivatives, investment banking, or capital-markets trading-system experience, and his database background leans toward MongoDB and MySQL rather than the SQL Server or Oracle required. None of the mandatory criteria are met at the required depth.
strengths: Active knowledge-sharer with conference talks, workshops, and open-source libraries, indicating solid communication skills
Exposure to CI/CD pipelines, Docker, and Java, which partially align with optional desirable skills
Brief professional C++ work at National Instruments on a real shipped product (LabWindows/CVI) shows baseline familiarity with the language
concerns: C++ professional experience is ~6 months versus the 5–10 years required; the vast majority of his career is TypeScript/Node.js/DLang
Zero fixed-income, derivatives, investment banking, or capital-markets domain knowledge present anywhere in the resume
No evidence of SQL Server or Oracle proficiency; database experience is limited to MongoDB and MySQL in web-service contexts
